description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar energy installation featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels elevated above a base structure. The solar panels are supported by a visible metal framework, likely made of steel or aluminum, with diagonal and vertical supports for stability. The base area beneath the panels appears to be covered with gravel or concrete, possibly for maintenance access or to prevent vegetation growth. The structure is compact and designed for urban or suburban environments, suitable for installation in parks, parking lots, or roadside areas. No visible electrical inverter or cabling is apparent, suggesting these components may be housed within the base or nearby enclosures. The overall design prioritizes durability and efficient solar energy capture.
description: A compact ground-mounted solar panel array on a sturdy metal frame with a gravel or concrete base, designed for urban energy generation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the solar panel array and the supporting frame structure beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the array and the angled support beams under the panels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panels and the symmetrical support structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the frame's depth and the base surface details.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal (likely aluminum or steel) frame and support posts
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 4 panels
